text:
Fascinating — look at the message count jump. Turn 1 had 1→3→5→7 messages. Then turn 2 started at **1,194 messages** — the full session history from turn 1 plus all the turn 2 context. The messages fluctuate (1194-1212) because the `-c` (continue) sessions overlap and the proxy captured from multiple parallel sessions.

The viz_data.json is 579MB which is too large for the browser. But the capture shows the multi-turn pattern clearly. Let me push and summarize:

tool_uses.name: Bash
tool_uses.input:
{"command": "# The viz_data is 579MB - too big to commit. Just note the run exists.
echo "run_20260407_182127: 110 iterations, 3 turns, 579MB viz_data (too large for git)" > claude_captures/run_20260407_182127_README.txt
git add claude_captures/run_20260407_182127_README.txt && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
add multi-turn capture readme (3-turn conversation, 110 iterations)

Turn 1: explore crates (7 msgs, 1 agent)
Turn 2: find complex functions (1194+ msgs, heavy Bash)
Turn 3: suggest refactoring (failed - context too large)
viz_data.json too large (579MB) for git

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push 2>&1 | tail -3", "description": "Commit readme only", "timeout": 30000}
